response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, and some genuine low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. However, the role demands hardware-centric expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears anywhere in the resume. His experience is almost entirely web platforms, product engineering, and developer tooling, making this a fundamental domain mismatch despite his seniority and years of experience.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, satisfying the bachelor's + 8 years qualification path
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second in DLang
Some C/C++ and embedded exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, audio DSP thesis work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, training/inference optimization, or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture expertise
No AI accelerator or deep-learning performance background
Strong general engineering seniority and low-latency systems work
